column1: # knob-prose-baseline.tsv — BURN-DOWN DEBT LEDGER for check-knob-prose.sh
# (temperloop#164/#169, item registry-config-lints), the prose counterpart to
# workflows/scripts/kernel/personal-token-denylist-baseline.tsv (same
# mechanism, same consumed-once semantics — see that file's header for the
# exemption-vs-baseline distinction it inherits):
#   * the `<!-- knob-prose:allow -->` per-line marker is for PERMANENT,
#     legitimately-literal prose (a doc line that must show a value);
#   * THIS file is for PRE-EXISTING violations of the D3 "prose names knobs,
#     never values" rule that are debt, not exceptions — every row here is
#     meant to be fixed and removed. The follow-up item
#     (prose-tunables-migration) rewrites each listed line to name-only
#     (dropping the restated default) and deletes its baseline row — the
#     goal is an EMPTY file, not a maintained one. Do not add new rows: fix
#     new violations at source (or, if genuinely legitimate, mark the line
#     with the allow-marker), don't grandfather them in.
# Format: <repo-relative path> TAB <knob name> TAB <registry default,
#         verbatim> TAB <exact matched line content>
# Matching (check-knob-prose.sh): a hit is suppressed only when its (file,
# name, default, line-content) tuple exactly matches a row here, and each
# row suppresses exactly ONE occurrence — so a genuinely NEW violation
# anywhere, including a new duplicate of an already-listed line, still fails
# the gate. A row whose line no longer matches (already fixed, or the file
# changed around it) simply goes unused — expected burn-down progress, not
# an error.
claude/commands/build.md
column4: - **5-hour quota gate — pause at the level boundary, auto-resume after reset.** After each level's merge gate, before advancing (Step 4e-quota), the run checks the remaining 5-hour usage quota via `quota-gate.sh` (which reads the snapshot `status-line.sh` persists to `~/.claude/rate-limits.json`). If the remaining quota is below `BUILD_QUOTA_PAUSE_PCT` (default **10%**, set in `workflows/scripts/build/build.config.sh`), the run **pauses at that clean boundary and auto-resumes after the window resets** — a background `sleep <wait_secs>` (the duration `quota-gate.sh` emits, = `reset_ts − now + buffer`), then re-check and continue — rather than getting cut off mid-merge in the next level. The signal **fails open**: a missing/stale/unreadable snapshot never pauses a run. This is a `blocking-now`-adjacent *self-throttle*, not a human gate — it interrupts nothing and asks nothing; it only defers the *next level's* work to after reset.

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: - **`{"action":"pause","wait_secs":S,"reset_ts":T,"remaining_pct":N}`** → the remaining 5h quota is below `BUILD_QUOTA_PAUSE_PCT` (default 10%). **Pause, then auto-resume in-session:**

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: - **Merge gate (Step 4) is regime-selected, not unconditionally modal.** A clean, disjoint, independent set is **timed** — it auto-merges after the window (`BUILD_MERGE_GATE_WINDOW`, default 300s) **unless an operator objects**, where absence of objection = consent (the walk-away case). A **structurally risky** set (Step 4b) is **modal** — it hard-blocks on an `AskUserQuestion` for explicit approval and is *never* timed-auto-merged.

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: If **none** of (a)/(b)/(c) holds, the set is **clean, disjoint, and independent → timed** — a same-file/disjoint-hunk advisory note (the (a) file-overlap probe firing without a hunk conflict) does **not** flip this; it is printed in the summary and the set stays timed. **Window:** `BUILD_MERGE_GATE_WINDOW` (seconds), **default 300 (5 min)**; **`BUILD_MERGE_GATE_WINDOW=0` forces every set to hard-block** — the always-modal kill-switch that restores the prior always-explicit-approval behavior. **The kill-switch is evaluated here, at gate-entry (4a), so it must be set BEFORE the gate fires to take effect.** Changing `BUILD_MERGE_GATE_WINDOW=0` *mid-window* does **not** preempt a countdown already running — once 4a has selected the timed regime and scheduled the wake, the only mid-window intervention is the objection mechanism (any operator response → modal, per 4b), not the env var. State the selected regime (and, when timed, the window) in the 4a summary so the operator knows whether a countdown is running.

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: - **Reuse the existing approval-poll / `ScheduleWakeup` mechanism** — the same self-paced wake loop `assess` Step 6 uses to watch a plan's `status`. Schedule a wake `BUILD_MERGE_GATE_WINDOW` seconds out (default 300); **encode the absolute deadline timestamp AND the announce timestamp in the `ScheduleWakeup` `prompt` itself** (not just working memory) so they survive context summarization across the window, exactly as the Step-6 poll encodes its 2h deadline. Announce the window and the set up front, **including any unanswered `## Questions` entries collected in 4a** so the operator can act on them within the window: *"Merging N PRs in 5 min unless you object — reply to cancel. P deferred questions will take their defaults at the deadline unless you answer: <one-line per entry with its default>."*

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: 2. **Timeout ceiling — `BUILD_QUEUE_TIMEOUT` (seconds, default 1800 = 30 min)** per selected PR's time in queue. **Encode the absolute deadline timestamp in the poll's `ScheduleWakeup` `prompt`** (same pattern as the 4b timed-gate window and assess Step 6), so it survives context summarization across the wait. **Headless one-shot runs (`FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1`) poll for `MERGED` in the FOREGROUND, not via `ScheduleWakeup` (#626)** — a blocking `gh pr view` poll loop **at a ≥30s interval** (the same floor `ci-poll.sh` enforces in 3g — no reason to poll tighter than the queue lands) bounded by `"$BUILD_HEADLESS_POLL_TIMEOUT"` per Bash call (under the ~10-min cap; chain another foreground poll if still queued and the session budget allows). On `state == MERGED` → 4d in-session. If the bound elapses with the PR still `OPEN`+enqueued (`--auto` armed, not `DIRTY`/`CONFLICTING`), do **not** dequeue and do **not** yield for a wake — leave it enqueued and **stop**: the `funnel-merge-pending` marker (#624) resumes next tick, re-checks, finds `MERGED`, and clears the marker. (The step-3 dequeue-and-fallback recovery still applies on a *confirmed* stall/conflict, just driven by the foreground bound rather than a backgrounded wake.)

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: - **`backend` probe (scripted — selects the 4b merge path).** Run `workflows/scripts/build/gate.sh backend <owner>/<repo>`. Closed outcome set: `{"outcome":"NATIVE"}` | `{"outcome":"MANAGED"}` | `{"outcome":"MANAGED","probe_failed":true}` (always exit 0). Mechanics (as built): the `BUILD_MERGE_BACKEND` knob (`build.config.sh`, default `auto`) short-circuits an explicit `native`/`managed` override **without probing**; under `auto` the script probes the branch ruleset (`repos/<owner>/<repo>/rules/branches/main`) for a `merge_queue` rule. **A probe failure resolves to `MANAGED`, never `NATIVE`** (fail-safe: MANAGED never silently arms an auto-merge nobody chose), flagged `probe_failed:true` so "no queue" and "couldn't tell" stay distinguishable — **surface `probe_failed:true` in the 4a summary line** (e.g. `backend: MANAGED (probe failed — could not read branch rules; set BUILD_MERGE_BACKEND=native if this repo has a queue)`), never swallow it. `NATIVE` → the 4b native-queue path; `MANAGED` → the 4b managed path.

column1: claude/commands/build.md
column4: **Headless one-shot runs (`FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1`) — run the poll in the FOREGROUND, never backgrounded (#626).** A `claude -p` headless session has **no re-invoke-on-background-completion loop**, so a backgrounded poll would yield and end the session before CI finishes — the bug that left funnel PRs green-but-never-merged (the full reason, why `FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1` is the marker, and why it is *narrower* than the `--unattended OR FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1` operator-absent predicate — the interactive walk-away `--unattended` case has a working loop and keeps the backgrounded/timed path: [[Decisions/foundation - build headless (claude -p) foreground merge — no re-invoke loop (#626)]]). All three #626 foreground branches (here, 4b timed gate, 4b MERGED-poll) key on `FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1`. When `FUNNEL_OPERATOR_ABSENT=1`, instead run `ci-poll.sh` as an ordinary **blocking** `Bash` call (it loops to a terminal outcome itself) with an explicit bounded `--timeout "$BUILD_HEADLESS_POLL_TIMEOUT"` (default 540s, under the ~10-min Bash foreground cap): `ci-poll.sh <owner>/<repo> <pr> --timeout "$BUILD_HEADLESS_POLL_TIMEOUT"`. The session itself is un-timeout'd, so if CI is still pending at the bound **re-issue the foreground poll once more before parking** (one retry within 3g, not zero — most CI finishes inside a second window); if it is *still* pending after that retry, park `[m]` with the `pr:` pointer and **stop this item** — the `funnel-merge-pending` hand-off marker (#624) resumes it next tick when CI is long-green. Do **not** yield expecting a re-invoke. (This branch is headless-only; operator-present runs keep the backgrounded two-sweep poll above unchanged.)

column1: claude/commands/sweep.md
column4: - **`pause`** (remaining 5h quota < `BUILD_QUOTA_PAUSE_PCT`, default 10%) → **pause and auto-resume in-session:** note the pause in the Step 4 report state (and, optionally, a one-line comment is unnecessary — singletons have no plan note), launch the wait as a **background** `sleep <wait_secs>` (`run_in_background: true`; foreground `sleep` is blocked), end the turn announcing the resume time, and on the wake re-invocation re-run `"$quotaGate"` (loop while still `pause`) then continue with the next issue. The per-issue loop is the clean, state-consistent boundary — no issue is left half-driven by a pause. Skip the gate after the **last** issue (nothing left to protect → fall through to Step 4).
